mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.5% and remains there for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following trigger activation are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.5% and remains elevated for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following trigger activation are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following joint trigger activation are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 118.5 signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors recognize improved profit margins.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above 118.5 and maintains level for at least 1 month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following trigger activation are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 100,000 new jobs per month combined with rising federal funds rate above 3.0% signals a tightening labor market with wage pressure that reduces corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth falls below 100,000 AND FEDFUNDS rises above 3.0%
regime_hint: monetary tightening with labor market slowdown
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint trigger activation are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 192.0 combined with FEDFUNDS below 2.5% signals monetary policy lagging inflation, creating real negative rates that erode equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following joint trigger activation are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,800 thousand units signals strong residential investment demand that drives construction employment and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the demand impulse propagates through the economy.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods when HOUST > 1,800 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below 117.0 combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) falling below 132,000 thousand signals simultaneous supply and labor contraction that reduces aggregate output capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 117.0 AND PAYEMS declines below 132,000 thousand in the same month
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following joint trigger activation are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.
